topic.name: Tax Incentives for Renewable Energy
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at providing tax credits and deductions to promote investment in renewable energy and energy efficiency technologies.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the maximum income tax rate on corporations
Restoring America's Competitiveness in Enterprise (RACE) Act of 2011
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2018, the tax credit for qualified fuel cell property.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2016 the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources.
Document 4: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to extend through 2012 grants for investment in renewable energy property
Defines 'qualified solar manufacturing property' as any tangible personal property purchased to re-equip, expand, or establish a manufacturing facility for producing solar energy equipment.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a tax credit for residential energy efficiency expenditures
Extend through 2011 the investment tax credit for solar energy and fuel cell property.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax deduction for a specified percentage of the cost of installing and maintaining mechanical insulation property.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2010 the increased expensing allowance for depreciable business assets.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the accessibility, affordability, and quality of healthcare services under Medicare and Medicaid programs.

Document 1: Safe Nursing and Patient Care Act of 2007 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to place limitations on mandatory overtime for nurses
establish civil money penalties for violations of this Act.
Document 2: Drug Rebate Equalization Act of 2007 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to reduce the costs of prescription drugs
Requires the state contract with a Medicaid managed care organization to provide that payment for covered outpatient drugs shall be subject to the same rebate.
Document 3: Strengthening the Health Care Safety Net Act of 2009 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to establish a disproportionate share hospital (DSH) redistribution pool
award demonstration grants to health access networks to improve access, quality, and continuity of care for uninsured individuals.
Document 4: Pharmacy Access Improvement (PhAIm) Act of 2007 - Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare)
Requires prompt payment of clean claims to pharmacies by prescription drug plans.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to authorize direct payment under part B to a pharmacy for certain covered compound drugs
for the effective use of an implanted infusion pump.
Document 6: Medicare Part D Long-Term Care Patient Protection Act of 2007 - Amends part D of title XVIII (Medicare)
to provide a long-term care pharmacy benefit under Medicare part D.
Document 7: Medicaid Prescription Drug Rebate Equalization Act of 2009 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to reduce the costs of prescription drugs
Requires also that capitation rates paid to the organization be based on actual cost experience related to rebates.

topic.name: Community and Career Education Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing educational opportunities and support services for students through community involvement and career pathways.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to consortia composed of a local educational agency and one or more community-based, nonprofit, or other public and private entities
provide access to such services to students, families, and the community.
Document 2: authorize the Director of the National Academic and Career Innovation Center to award certain planning, implementation, transitional, and supplemental grants
create and enhance educational and career pathways for high school students to postsecondary schools
Document 3: increase requirements for grant recipients under the Gaining Early Awareness and Readiness for Undergraduate Programs (GEAR UP)
provide academic and mental health counseling services to participating students.
Document 4: authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to eligible entities to assist public elementary or secondary schools to function as full-service community schools
participate in community-based efforts to coordinate educational, developmental, family, health, and other comprehensive services.
Document 5: provide up to $17,500 of loan forgiveness under the Federal Family Education Loan and William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan programs
to highly qualified elementary and secondary school teachers who are employed in a teacher shortage area.
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states, high-need local educational agencies
to improve access to, and the sharing and use of, education data to improve student outcomes.
Document 7: establish a federal-state partnership to promote state investment in public higher education
to reduce tuition costs and support the enrollment, retention, and degree completion of low-income students at public institutions of higher education.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, focusing on temporary duty suspensions and modifications for various goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to modify the article description of cast stainless steel single-piece exhaust gas manifolds
extend temporarily the reduction of duty on such articles.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on light emitting diode (LED) down light modules.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain reusable fabric [cotton] bags.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on ceiling fans for permanent installation.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on cellular plastic sheets for micron-retention filters.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on staple fibers of viscose rayon.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on Basic Red 1.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving health care access, treatment, and benefits for veterans.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to accept as sufficient proof of service-connection of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
Requires the Secretary to resolve every reasonable doubt in favor of the veteran.
Document 2: Authorizes the payment of an additional special monthly pension to a veteran already eligible for a basic pension
if such veteran either: (1) has an additional disability or disabilities rated at 60% or more.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse any non-VA medical provider
Defines an 'eligible veteran' as a veteran who is enrolled in the VA patient enrollment system.
Document 4: Provides that, if a Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical facility is unable to provide such services for at least 180 days due to such disaster
the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall contract with one or more non-VA facilities.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to ensure that veterans eligible for hospital care have access to at least one full-service VA hospital in the state
Directs the Secretary to report to Congress on compliance with such requirement.
Document 6: requires the U.S. Postal Service to issue and sell a Veterans Health Care Stamp to help fund the medical care and treatment of veterans.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from altering the health care available to a veteran
based solely on the length of time since he or she last received health care from the VA.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Exploration Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative documents focused on the exploration, development, and regulation of oil and gas resources in the United States, particularly in coastal and environmentally sensitive areas.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock in carrying out any Act or program to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to repeal the requirement that the Secretary of the Interior approve plans for geological and geophysical (including oil and natural gas) explorations in the outer Continental Shelf within 30 days after submission.
Document 3: Authorizes the exploration, leasing, development, production, and economically feasible and prudent transportation of oil and gas in and from the Coastal Plain of Alaska.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a competitive oil and gas leasing program for oil and gas exploration, development, and production on the Coastal Plain.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct lease sale 220 within one year after enactment of this Act, and include at least two lease sales in the Virginia lease sale planning area in each five-year oil and gas leasing program.
Prohibits any oil or natural gas exploration, development, or production off the Virginia coast that would conflict with a military operation.
Document 6: Requires holders of leases authorizing exploration and production of oil or natural gas to develop them diligently in order to ensure timely production from the lease.

Amends the Mineral Leasing Act and the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to require a lessee, as a condition of such a lease, to pay an annual fee of $4 per acre on the acres covered by a non-producing lease.
Document 7: Declares without force or effect all federal prohibitions against the expenditure of appropriated funds to conduct natural gas leasing and preleasing activities for any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS).
Revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands from leasing for natural gas exploration and production.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming U.S. immigration policy, focusing on entry requirements, asylum procedures, and border security measures.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize a Department of Homeland Security official to waive certain entry documentary requirements
sets forth additional requirements for such entries.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to prohibit the government from bearing any expense of counsel for any person in a removal or related appeal proceedings
Provides for additional immigration judges and U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement attorneys.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to permit a lawful alien who enters the United States on a nonimmigrant student visa
to attend a U.S. public secondary school for longer than one year.
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding the visa waiver program
authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate program countries.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act provide that the initial admission period for an alien with a valid machine-readable biometric border crossing identification card
Prohibits the admission or admission extension of such an alien if...
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security... to increase the number of full-time Customs and Border Protection (CBP) officers
to improve border security and for other purposes.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the definition of 'aggravated felony' a third conviction for driving while intoxicated
Makes inadmissible or deportable an alien who has been convicted of driving while intoxicated.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons Accountability
topic.description: This topic focuses on the assessment and reporting of foreign countries' programs related to electromagnetic pulse weapons and their delivery systems.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on the designation, management, and boundary adjustments of wilderness areas and federal lands in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to adjust the boundaries of the North Cascades National Park
Requires lands in the newly designated wilderness to include lands along the route of the Steheken Valley Road
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to the local communities in South Dakota that are currently managing certain community cemeteries all interest of the United States
Requires the conveyed lands to continue to be used in the same manner and for the same purposes as they were used immediately before their conveyance
Document 3: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, and to be known as the Pine Forest Range Wilderness
Sets forth provisions for the management of such land, including regarding livestock grazing, water rights, Native American tribal rights
Document 4: This bill amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to redesignate the Olympic Wilderness as the 'Daniel J. Evans Wilderness'
Document 5: Designates specified Bureau of Land Management (BLM) and National Forest System lands in California as wilderness areas
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to study connecting the northern and southern parts of the Los Padres National Forest using a trail corridor
Document 6: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness
Provides for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land within the Park in such Wilderness
Document 7: Authorizes the Department of the Army to convey all right, title, and interest of the United States in certain real property to the city of Copperas Cove, Texas
Requires the city, in consideration for such conveyance, to convey all its right, title, and interest in real properties of equal fair market value

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving child welfare, preventing abuse and trafficking, and enhancing support systems for vulnerable children.

Document 1: authorize the use of grants to local educational agencies for carrying out child sexual abuse awareness and prevention programs
establish principles of effectiveness applicable to these grant programs
Document 2: require consultation with state and local elected officials and a public hearing before awarding grants
provide non-federal housing facilities for unaccompanied alien children
Document 3: require the procedures a state must have in effect for the orderly and timely interstate placement of children
Grants are authorized for development of a centralized electronic system
Document 4: develop guidelines for use by state child welfare agencies in training appropriate child welfare employees
recommendations on how state child welfare agencies may prevent children from becoming victims of trafficking
Document 5: revise the grant program for juvenile and family court personnel
to improve training, education, technical assistance, and research to assist courts
Document 6: states should treat minor victims of sex trafficking as crime victims
funds awarded under the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program should be used to provide education, training, deterrence
Document 7: authorize the United States Marshals Service to investigate cases involving a sex offender
cases involving a missing child

topic.name: Mental Health and Substance Abuse Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at improving mental health services and addressing substance abuse issues across different populations.

Document 1: establish a five-year grant program to improve the monitoring of VA mental health and substance abuse treatment programs
investigate and monitor VA facilities care and treatment of veterans with mental illness or substance abuse issues.
Document 2: authorize the use of specified funds to provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals with mental illness
support services for individuals released from inpatient or residential institutions.
Document 3: require the Director of the National Institutes of Health to take certain actions regarding eating disorder research
establish a CDC clearinghouse for the collection and storage of data generated under this Act.
Document 4: develop a plan to address the service needs of juveniles with mental health or substance abuse disorders

requires states receiving juvenile justice grants to include in their state plan information regarding screening, assessment, and treatment of juveniles with mental health and substance abuse disorders.
Document 5: set forth criteria for the certification of federally-qualified community behavioral health centers
authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award matching grants to states or Indian tribes for mental health and substance abuse services.
Document 6: establish a National Mental Health Policy Laboratory to identify and implement policy changes
award planning grants to enable states to carry out demonstration programs to improve the provision of behavioral health services.
Document 7: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to initiate and sustain mental health first aid training programs
training on action plans and protocols for referral to mental health services.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating drug safety, ensuring access to investigational treatments, and addressing the prescribing practices for controlled substances.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to permit an investigational drug...
Establishes a procedure for accelerated approval of an investigational drug, biological product, or device that is reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit...
Document 2: This bill requires the Department of Health and Human Services to provide information to prescribers in federally qualified health centers...
best practices for prescribing naloxone for patients a provider identifies as having an elevated risk of opioid overdose.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to establish a streamlined data review program...
the holder of an approved application for a drug or biological product may submit a summary of clinical data to support approval of the drug for the treatment of cancer...
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish the Center for Postmarket Evaluation and Research for Drugs and Biologics...
conducting postmarket risk assessment and surveillance of such drugs and products;
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem to be adulterated any unfinished dextromethorphan that is possessed, received, or distributed in violation of this Act.
Prohibits a person from possessing or receiving unfinished dextromethorphan unless the person is registered with the Secretary of Health and Human Services...
Document 6: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that anyone within the United States who enters into a conspiracy to possess or traffic in controlled substances outside the United States...
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Advanced Diagnostics Education Council to recommend standard terms and definitions related to innovative diagnostics...

Extends the exclusivity period for a drug or biological product if the diagnostic test related to such drug has been determined by the Secretary to have been developed by, or with the participation of, the manufacturer or sponsor of the drug...

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation and projects focused on water rights, ecosystem restoration, and water recycling initiatives in the United States.

Document 1: Bill Williams River Water Rights Settlement Act of 2014
Hualapai Tribe Bill Williams River Water Rights Settlement Agreement
Document 2: Avra/Black Wash Reclamation and Riparian Restoration Project
water recycling facilities and riparian habitat restoration
Document 4: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007
ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality
Document 5: San Gabriel Watershed Restoration Act
feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control
Document 6: South Orange County Recycled Water Enhancement Act
design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility

topic.name: Mortgage Assistance and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and programs aimed at modifying mortgage requirements, providing assistance to homeowners, and regulating mortgage securities.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements under the HOPE for Homeowners Program
Increases from 90% to 93% of a property's appraised value the cap on the principal obligation amount of a refinanced eligible insured mortgage.
Document 2: Establishes a Federal Board of Certification to certify that the mortgages within a security instrument meet the underlying standards
The purpose of this certification process is to increase the transparency, predictability, and reliability of securitized mortgage products.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the limitation on the mortgage interest deduction for the acquisition indebtedness
Acquisition indebtedness is indebtedness that is incurred in acquiring, constructing, or substantially improving any qualified residence.
Document 4: Sets a deadline for the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) to terminate the conservatorship of either Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac
Increases the minimum capital level required for each enterprise.
Document 5: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 to require servicers of federally related mortgage loans to establish for each borrower's account a single electronic record
Prescribes requirements for the affordable loan modification process.
Document 6: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to direct the Secretary to require servicers of the underlying mortgages to take advantage of the HOPE for Homeowners Program
Requires lenders to participate in HOPE for Homeowners Program when homeowners elect to participate.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish a mortgage emergency assistance program
Prohibits a mortgagee from accelerating the maturity of or commencing any legal action regarding a mortgage.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation and restoration of coral reef ecosystems through funding, research, and community-based initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function from vessel impacts
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
NOAA must submit a report to Congress describing all activities undertaken to implement the strategy
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
Modifies the goals and objectives of the national coral reef action strategy
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities to conserve coral reef ecosystems
Document 6: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects
The U.S. Coral Reef Task Force is authorized to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems

topic.name: Support for Beginning Farmers
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing assistance, resources, and programs to support beginning farmers and ranchers in the agricultural sector.

Document 1: Beginning farmer and rancher development grants available for energy conservation and organic and value-added alternative production.
Makes socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers eligible for inventory sales preferences.
Document 5: Modifies and establishes programs to assist beginning farmers.
Reauthorizes the Beginning Farmer and Rancher Development Program.

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies aimed at promoting renewable energy sources, regulating alternative fuels, and addressing environmental impacts associated with energy production and consumption.

Document 1: qualify new flexible fuel hybrid motor vehicles for the alternative motor vehicle tax credit
capable of operating on an alternative fuel, on gasoline, and on any blend thereof
Document 2: prohibit a federal agency from being required to consider the social cost of carbon
as part of any cost-benefit or regulatory analysis
Document 3: Cutting Federal Red Tape to Facilitate Renewable Energy Act
consider and analyze at the election of the applicant only the proposed action alternative
Document 4: International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) Act of 2008
support governments in establishing policies and programs that promote renewable energy
Document 5: amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel standard program
the renewable fuel that is required to be blended into gasoline must be advanced biofuel
Document 6: amends the Clean Air Act to repeal the renewable fuel standard
repeal a requirement that the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency assess and report
Document 7: establish a program to provide loan guarantees for the construction of facilities for the manufacture of advanced vehicle batteries
provide grants to state governments, local governments, metropolitan transportation authorities

topic.name: Data Breach Notification and Security
topic.description: The documents focus on various legislative acts aimed at ensuring timely notification and security measures for breaches involving personally identifiable information in health care and federal agencies.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security.
theft of or unlawful access to the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), following the discovery of a breach of the personally identifiable information of an individual to notify: (1) such individual within one hour.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress annually on: (1) all such breaches that occurred within the past year.
Document 3: Requires any federal agency or business entity... to notify: (1) any U.S. resident whose information has been... accessed or acquired.
Requires notifications to be made without unreasonable delay following the discovery of a security breach.
Document 4: Requires any entity that collects... personally identifiable information to: (1) provide consumers notice before using personally identifiable information for purposes unrelated to the transaction.
implement an information security policy that is applicable to the information security practices.
Document 5: Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised as a result of a breach.
Requires federal agency information security programs to include data breach notification procedures.
Document 6: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security.
known to have resulted in the theft or unlawful access of the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 7: directs... a federal agency that experiences a compromise in its computer systems resulting in the loss of personally identifiable information to provide to any individual directly affected a one-year license of such product.

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Money Laundering Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing tax fraud and money laundering through various regulatory measures and protections for taxpayers.

Document 1: Directs the Inspector General for Tax Administration of the Department of the Treasury to audit the Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
Prohibits the obligation or expenditure of any amounts to carry out the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act until the report of the IRS audit is submitted to Congress.
Document 2: Requires the Department of the Treasury to establish a plan to reduce the administrative time required to process and resolve cases of tax-related identity theft
Imposes restrictions on the use of prepaid debit cards for tax refunds.
Document 3: Amends provisions of federal law known as the Bank Secrecy Act of 1970 relating to money laundering violations
Grants independent legal authority to the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) of the Department of the Treasury to bring legal action to enforce anti-money laundering laws.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code relating to money laundering to allow a conviction for money laundering based on merely concealing or disguising monetary instruments
Expand the concept of monetary proceeds to include gross receipts.
Document 5: Amends the federal criminal code to redefine 'specified unlawful activity' for purposes of money laundering provisions
Increases the penalty for bulk cash smuggling in or out of the United States from 5 to 10 years' imprisonment.
Document 6: Requires the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to submit to Congress a report including an assessment of the feasibility of notifying a taxpayer when a return has been filed for the taxpayer
Recommendations with respect to any infrastructure or other changes that would facilitate the notification process.
Document 7: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to disseminate information on mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud targeting seniors
Directs the Attorney General (DOJ) to establish and administer a competitive grant program for mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud prevention education programs for senior citizens.

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating and overseeing banking institutions and their activities to ensure financial stability and consumer protection.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to apply the bank share or asset acquisition requirements of the Bank Holding Company Act
Subjects an industrial bank to Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) requirements regarding registration, reporting, and bank examinations.
Document 2: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision
FSOC must make these final determinations using an indicator-based measurement approach established by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision.
Document 3: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 with respect to the prohibition against a banking entity's engaging in proprietary trading
Exempts from the Volcker Rule any banking entity with total consolidated assets of $10 billion or less.
Document 4: Prohibits a federal banking agency from suggesting, requesting, or ordering a depository institution to terminate a specific customer account
Prescribes requirements for notice from the federal banking agency to the depository institution about such a customer account restriction.
Document 5: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to declare that no person shall be considered a fiduciary to an employee benefit plan
Declares further that no swap dealer shall be considered to act as an advisor to a Special Entity solely by reason of providing information.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prescribe requirements for deposit restricted qualified tuition programs
Denies treatment as a security under the Securities Act of 1933.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company
Prohibits any individual who is an officer, director, partner, or employee of any securities entity from serving simultaneously as an officer of any insured depository institution.

topic.name: Endangered Species Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection, recovery, and management of endangered and threatened species in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
require the Department of the Interior to make available online a searchable database
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code
make it unlawful to knowingly sponsor or exhibit an animal in an animal fighting venture
Document 3: amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
prioritize the consideration of petitions to list a species as endangered or threatened
Document 4: amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
require the Secretary of the Interior to publish a summary statement of the scientific basis for such determination
Document 5: amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972
authorize the Secretary to issue one-year permits for the lethal taking of sea lions
Document 6: directs the Secretary of Agriculture or the Secretary of the Interior to give effect to a statewide conservation and management plan
prohibits any proposed action pursuant to the National Environmental Policy Act from being denied solely on the basis of...
Document 7: amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
make the Act's provisions regarding the determination of endangered or threatened species inapplicable to specific salamanders

topic.name: Government Legislation and Designations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to designating properties, managing resources, and authorizing federal assistance and programs.

Document 1: Designates Albania, Croatia, Georgia, Macedonia, and Ukraine as eligible to receive assistance under the NATO Participation Act of 1994.
Authorizes FY2008 appropriations for: (1) Albania; (2) Croatia; (3) Georgia; (4) Macedonia; and (5) Ukraine.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to Wyoming, upon state request, without consideration and by quitclaim deed, all interest of the United States in approximately 10 acres of National Forest System land.

Repeals the requirement that Ranch A be used for: (1) fish and wildlife management and educational activities; and (2) maintenance and restoration of the museum-quality real and personal property there.
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4551 East 52nd Street in Odessa, Texas, as the 'Staff Sergeant Marvin 'Rex' Young Post Office Building.'
Document 4: This bill authorizes the Library of Congress to purchase, maintain, or replace reproducers for books published either in raised characters, on sound-reproduction recordings, or in any other form for the use of the blind and for other physically disabled U.S. residents.
Document 5: Directs the Department of the Interior to convey to the municipality of Anchorage, Alaska, in response to its offer to acquire, the reversionary interests of the United States in various sites.
Requires the municipality to pay the appraised value of the federal reversionary interest and all costs associated with the conveyance.
Document 6: Requires the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to increase the supplemental education fee charged to a prospective employer for each CNMI-Only Transitional Worker who is issued a permit during the transition period to U.S. immigration law.
Caps the number of permits issued for such nonimmigrant workers for FY2017 at 15,000.
Document 7: This bill requires the Department of Commerce Office of Security to manage the law enforcement and security programs of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) directly.
The Government Accountability Office shall report to Congress, one year after enactment of this bill, on the costs and performance of the NIST Police Services Group.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations aimed at improving transportation infrastructure, safety standards, and passenger services in the rail sector.

Document 1: Requires at least 25% of amounts appropriated to the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) for capital grants to states for rail line relocation and improvement projects to be used for high-speed rail or associated projects.
Document 2: Revises positive train control systems implementation requirements for Class I railroad carriers that regularly provide intercity or commuter rail passenger transportation over their main lines.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to revise federal regulations regarding DOT-111 tank cars used to move flammable liquids to improve tank car design standards.
Document 4: Directs the National Railroad Passenger Corporation (Amtrak) to propose a pet policy that allows passengers to transport domesticated cats or dogs on certain Amtrak trains.
Document 5: Amends the Transportation Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (TIFIA) to make eligible for TIFIA funding any projects to improve or construct public infrastructure located within walking distance of and accessible to a fixed guideway transit facility.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to identify and encourage the use of advanced technologies, including 3-dimensional modeling and simulation software, during environmental, planning, financial management, design, simulation, and construction processes of federal-aid highway projects.
Document 7: Requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan, meeting specified requirements and approved by the Surface Transportation Board, for any of its rail cars that it stores for three or more years.

topic.name: Nuclear Energy and STEM Initiatives

Targets funding rules for single-employer defined benefit retirement plans.
Document 7: Expands COBRA coverage to include domestic partners and their dependent children.
Amends multiple acts to ensure equal access to health insurance continuation benefits.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation

topic.name: Indian Trust Land Management

topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the safety, labeling, and regulation of food products in the United States.
